characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Buried Alive: The evil sorcerer does this to Aladdin, sealing up the magic cave and leaving Aladdin trapped down below when Aladdin does not hand over the lamp. Delinquents: Aladdin is this at the start of the story, playing about with other kids on the streets of the Chinese city where he lives. Even Bad Men Love Their Mamas: Aladdin turns to petty crime, but his first instinct is always to make sure his mother is well cared for. Not that this stops him using her in his schemes. Guile Hero: Aladdin... eventually. He escapes his first misadventure by instinctively knowing his 'uncle' can't be trusted, and slowly develops the cunning to read the situation and act accordingly. Idiot Ball:Aladdin stupidly leaves the magic lamp out where anyone can get at it.Then he forgets that he still has the magic ring, and is on the verge of drowning himself in the river when he accidentally rubs it and brings forth the other genie again.

characters.name: Aladdin
characters.description: Buried Alive: The evil sorcerer does this to Aladdin, sealing up the magic cave and leaving Aladdin trapped down below when Aladdin does not hand over the lamp. Delinquents: Aladdin is this at the start of the story, playing about with other kids on the streets of the Chinese city where he lives. Even Bad Men Love Their Mamas: Aladdin turns to petty crime, but his first instinct is always to make sure his mother is well cared for. Not that this stops him using her in his schemes. Guile Hero: Aladdin... eventually. He escapes his first misadventure by instinctively knowing his 'uncle' can't be trusted, and slowly develops the cunning to read the situation and act accordingly. Idiot Ball:Aladdin stupidly leaves the magic lamp out where anyone can get at it.Then he forgets that he still has the magic ring, and is on the verge of drowning himself in the river when he accidentally rubs it and brings forth the other genie again.

characters.name: Aladdin's mother
characters.description: Adapted Out: Notably, whereas most adaptations of the story often feature her, she's omitted from the 1992 Disney film adaptation, presumably to make Aladdin more of an underdog figure. Everyone Has Standards: She's incredibly uncomfortable with Aladdin's use of djinn at first, believing them to be evil creatures. Faint in Shock: She does this when she sees the djinn of the lamp for the first time. Good Parents: She looks after Aladdin, as shown when Aladdin becomes comatose after fainting from starvation. Once he recovers, she starts supplying him with all the food she can and cautions him to slow himself. Modest Royalty: How she catches the sultan's attention: though dressed like a pauper, she shows up every day at the audience until he finally wonders why she is there. Once called forth, she presents him with a big basket of enormous jewels, asking for her son to marry the princess.

characters.name: Mustapha
characters.description: Death by Despair: This is how he died. Deceased Parents Are the Best: He wasn'tquitea particularly effective parent to Aladdin, but he didtry. Disappeared Dad: Aladdin's father, a tailor, died of disappointment after Aladdin preferred being a juvenile delinquent to following him into the tailoring business. No Name Given: In some translations of the story, he's often referred to as "So-and-so the tailor" and as a result, some other adaptations, like the1990 television film, have followed suit, with characters referring to his wife as "Mrs So-and-so." Posthumous Character: He's already dead by the time the story starts.

characters.name: Sorcerer
characters.description: Big Bad: He spends the story trying to steal the lamp, attempts to kill Aladdin and kidnaps Princess Badroulboudour along with the palace. Ethnic Magician: The evil sorcerer is Moroccan, while the main characters are Chinese. Or at least Uyghurs or some other west Chinese Muslim ethnic group since ethnic Han Chinese are not really a Muslim people. Insane Proprietor: When Aladdin is out, the sorcerer disguises himself as an old woman exchanging old lamps for new ones. This creates a commotion, attracting the princess' attention. Then one of her handmaidens mentions they have an old beat-up lamp in their palace, and exchanges the genie's lamp for a regular lamp. Nice Job Fixing It, Villain!: Prior entering the cave, he gives Aladdin a lesser Djinn in a ring to help him traverse the magic cave where the lamp was hidden, and quickly forgets about him. This bites him in the back twice, as Aladdin uses him to escape the cave when he traps him inside, and uses the ring again to locate the evil sorcerer and travel directly to his location. No Name Given: In the oldest versions of the story and most straight adaptations of them, his name is never stated. Inpantomimeversions he's usually namedAbanazar, while inthe Disney versionhe's Jafar.

characters.name: Jinns
characters.description: All Wishes Granted:Zig-Zagged. There are very few limitations to the genies' wishes: The genie of the ring cannot cancel wishes granted by the genie of the lamp, and when Aladdin is tricked into asking for the egg of a Roc Bird, the genie makes it very clear that not only will it not happen, the only reason Aladdin is still alive after asking is because the genie knows Aladdin was tricked into it. Berserk Button: The genies grant Aladdin's every last wish, but when he asks for a roc's egg (after the princess asks him, after the sorcerer tells her about it) the genie of the lamp blows up at him, only sparing him because it was not his idea. Chekhov's Boomerang: In addition to the genie of the lamp, Aladdin holds a magic ring containing a lesser genie. The second genie gets him out of the cave in which he wasBuried Aliveand also helps him recover his lamp, palace, and wife near the end of the story when an evil sorcerer has stolen them. Genie in a Bottle: Both the genie of the lamp and the genie of the ring are bound to serve whoever possesses their containers.

characters.name: Princess Badroulboudour
characters.description: Adaptational Name Change: Her name is often changed in adaptations of the story, since her original name doesn't really roll off the tongue. Daddy's Girl: The princess is the Sultan's favorite daughter — quite possibly his only daughter — and they are very attached to each other. Go Seduce My Archnemesis: At Aladdin's urging, the princess pretends to be in love with theEvil Sorcererwho has taken her captive. She gets him drunk enough to lower his suspicions and then poisons him. Meaningful Name: Her name means "full moon of full moons", referring to her great beauty. Satellite Love Interest: She's not given much characterisation beyond being Aladdin's love interest. However, she is given the characteristic of being crafty and witty and is able to successfully trick the African wizard intoaccidentally poisoning himself.

characters.name: Vizier
characters.description: Cassandra Truth: He is the only one who figures out that Aladdin's riches and the incredible things he can do — like build an enormous palace in a single night — must be the result of shady magic. The sultan writes him off as being jealous because the vizier's son was thrown over for Aladdin. Evil Chancellor: Though not the main villain, the Grand Vizier is still something of aJerkassObstructive Bureaucratwho wants to get rid of Aladdin so that his own son can marry the princess. (Of course,Jerkass Has a Point; see the entry forCassandra Truthabove.)

characters.name: Sorcerer's brother
characters.description: Adapted Out: Unlike his brother, he's often omitted from adaptations of the story, presumably to keep them from getting too overlong. Avenging the Villain: He tries to do this, by planning to kill Aladdin by tricking him into making him wish for the egg of a roc, knowing it will infuriate the Djinns. Dead Person Impersonation: The sorcerer's brother kills and impersonates the wise old woman Fatimah. Disguised in Drag: The evil sorcerer's more evil brother murders an old holy woman and disguises himself as her in order to worm his way into Aladdin's home. The genie of the lamp is not fooled. Eviler than Thou: He's even worse than his brother.
